content: A differentiated result-code vocabulary — specialists report 'completed', auditors report 'passed'/'rejected', coordinators report 'complete'/'blocked'/'revision-loop' — enables precise routing decisions in creative pipelines. The 6 distinct result values ('complete', 'completed', 'passed', 'blocked', 'failed', 'revision-loop') mapped unambiguously to routing actions across 163 routes. This vocabulary worked cleanly because each agent type uses a disjoint result-code set, preventing ambiguity in routing conditions.
sourceEvidence: Category 3 (Routing Completeness): '163 routes across 20 routing agents. Result code vocabulary is intentionally differentiated: specialists report "completed", auditors report "passed"/"rejected", coordinators report "complete"/"blocked"/"revision-loop". All paths terminate. All convergence bounds present and documented.'

history.reason: Cold start — first observation from initial factory run
